executive_summary: Latest updates from 3 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 3 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: Morning update. Netanyahu says he won’t ‘put a schedule’ on end to war as Trump makes new threat. Trump tells aides he is willing to end Iran war without reopening Hormuz: Report. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: Netanyahu says he won’t ‘put a schedule’ on end to war as Trump makes new threat
key_developments.detail: Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has said the war with Iran has crossed the halfway mark but declined to give a timeline for when it may end, in remarks to US network Newsmax. “It’s definitely beyond the halfway point. key_developments.headline: Trump tells aides he is willing to end Iran war without reopening Hormuz: Report
key_developments.detail: US President Donald Trump has told aides he is willing to end the military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, according to a Wall Street Journal report citing administration officials. terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
